job_title: Business Intelligence Analyst for the Division of Data Management and Technology
company: New York City Department of Housing Preservation & Development
strengths: Power BI dashboard development with quantified impact
End-to-end ETL and data pipeline building
Advanced Excel and data visualization skills
weaknesses: Limited tenure in formal BI roles (≈2 years)
No explicit SQL Developer tool listed
improvement_bullets: Collaborated with cross-functional teams to migrate legacy reporting into modern BI platforms (Power BI, Oracle BI), acting as a subject matter expert to train staff on advanced dashboard functionality and data storytelling.
Engineered scalable data pipelines and reporting solutions using SQL Developer to extract and transform complex datasets, ensuring high-quality, actionable insights for organizational leadership.

Designed and documented comprehensive Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for data intake, cleaning, and BI platform usage, reducing technical onboarding time by 50% and ensuring consistent data governance.
Utilized Microsoft Access to maintain and query relational databases, streamlining data integration between departmental software systems and supporting agency-wide business intelligence initiatives.

Partnered with project stakeholders to evaluate data needs and translate business objectives into technical specifications for new analytic models, directly supporting informed policy and operational decision-making.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Business Intelligence Analyst for the Division of Data Management and Technology opportunity with the New York City Department of Housing Preservation & Development. I am interested in this opportunity because I am deeply committed to using data-driven insights to improve public infrastructure and essential city services. What interests me about working for HPD is its focus on maintaining building safety and fostering affordable housing, which inspires me because it directly impacts the quality of life for all New Yorkers. I believe my expertise in BI platform development and my ability to translate complex data into actionable operational strategies would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by prioritizing clear communication between technical teams and non-technical stakeholders. In fact, while at Shine Masters Cleaning Services, I identified that manual reporting was hindering decision-making. I developed and deployed automated Power BI dashboards and streamlined data workflows, which reduced processing time by 40% and enabled executive leadership to shift focus toward high-value customer segments, resulting in a 300% increase in acquisition over 12 months. Ultimately, I know that the Business Intelligence Analyst for the Division of Data Management and Technology role at the New York City Department of Housing Preservation & Development would allow me to bring my advanced data visualization skills to the critical mission of tracking ENS performance. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my SQL and data integration skills. I worked closely with Underwriting and Product teams to reconcile insurance data using complex SQL queries and PostgreSQL databases, improving data accuracy by 30%. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my technical proficiency in SQL and Power BI in working on the improvement of agency-wide BI initiatives at HPD. I know that my expertise in building robust data pipelines and my dedication to collaborative, team-oriented problem-solving align with what it takes to succeed at the Business Intelligence Analyst for the Division of Data Management and Technology role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
